sections.heading: What is a Velocity Calculator?
sections.content: A Velocity Calculator is a free online tool that calculates the speed or velocity of an object using physics formulas. It can compute average velocity using distance and time or final velocity for accelerated motion using v = u + a × t.

subSections.heading: Open the Calculator
subSections.content: Access the page in your browser.

subSections.heading: Select Calculation Type
subSections.content: Choose simple velocity (distance/time) or accelerated motion (v = u + a × t).

subSections.heading: Enter Values
subSections.content: Input distance, time, initial velocity, and acceleration as needed.

subSections.heading: Check Units
subSections.content: Ensure all units are consistent for accurate results.

subSections.heading: Educational Tool
subSections.content: Helps understand velocity, acceleration, and motion.

subSections.heading: Hobbyists & DIY Projects
subSections.content: Calculate motion for model vehicles or experiments.

subSections.heading: Engineers & Designers
subSections.content: Quick estimation of velocity for simple projects.

subSections.heading: Travel & Daily Calculations
subSections.content: Determine speed from distance and time.

subSections.heading: Example 1 – Constant Velocity
subSections.content: Distance: 100 meters | Time: 20 seconds | Velocity: 100 ÷ 20 = 5 m/s

subSections.heading: Example 2 – Accelerated Motion
subSections.content: Initial Velocity (u): 0 m/s | Acceleration (a): 2 m/s² | Time (t): 10 seconds | Final Velocity (v): 0 + 2 × 10 = 20 m/s

faq.a: No, it works directly in your browser.
